raw_13_history_summary.summary: Repo policy is consistent with raw 13 git-hook-ban: ZERO active hooks, all enforcement is user-explicit-invocation. Prior tools (raw_enforce.sh, atlas_precommit_check.sh, py_ban_guard.hexa) document hook integration as POSSIBLE BUT REQUIRES EXPLICIT USER AUTHORIZATION; none auto-install. The 2026-04-26 githooks-recovery cycle treated hook restoration as user-driven, not tool-driven. raw 13 is not literally text-cited but is consistently HONORED in practice.
raw_13_history_summary.implication_for_this_kick: Federation orchestrator must be invocable as `hexa run tool/pre_commit_federation.hexa --staged` from CLI or CI; MUST NOT write .git/hooks/* under any path; install requires (a) raw 13 lift AND (b) explicit user --install-hook flag with confirmation prompt.

rows.sentinel_format: (stderr text) [py_ban_guard] BLOCK — R37/AN13/L3-PY: staged .py 발견  (NO __PY_BAN_GUARD__ machine sentinel — design recommends adding one)
rows.origin_cycle: 2026-04-18 BG agent pod2 .py incident — raw#8 enforcement (#raw8)

exit_codes.0: all witnesses meet 18-field minimum + canonical names + types
all carry_forward entries cite parent_cycle + cycle slug

federation_pipeline_spec.orchestrator_file: tool/pre_commit_federation.hexa  (DESIGNED, NOT IMPL — separate impl cycle required)
federation_pipeline_spec.orchestrator_role: Single entry point that fans out to the 7 standalone lints; aggregates sentinels; emits a federation sentinel; propagates exit code per mode.
invocation_modes: hexa run tool/pre_commit_federation.hexa --root /path              (full-tree audit, CI nightly)
hexa run tool/pre_commit_federation.hexa --selftest                (each lint's --selftest in sequence)
hexa run tool/pre_commit_federation.hexa --only L1,L3              (subset; comma-separated lint_ids)
hexa run tool/pre_commit_federation.hexa --skip L2                 (disable specific lints — see honesty section)

fast_fail.description: Stop at first failing lint; surface its sentinel verbatim then federation sentinel with verdict=FAIL.
fast_fail.use_case: Local dev pre-commit; minimize wait time.

report_all.description: Run all lints regardless of failures; aggregate violations across all; emit federation sentinel last.

execution_modes.selection: Toggle via env NEXUS_FEDERATION_MODE=fast_fail|report_all (default per scope above).

sentinel_aggregation.example_pass: __PRE_COMMIT_FEDERATION__ PASS lints=7 ran=7 violations=0 failed_lints= mode=report_all
sentinel_aggregation.example_fail: __PRE_COMMIT_FEDERATION__ FAIL lints=7 ran=3 violations=5 failed_lints=L1,L3 mode=fast_fail
sentinel_aggregation.ordering: Per-lint sentinels are echoed in run-order BEFORE the federation sentinel; federation sentinel is always the LAST line on stdout.

exit_code_propagation.1: Any lint FAIL (federation verdict=FAIL). Specific lint exit codes preserved in stderr per-lint summary; federation rc is max(per-lint rc) clipped to {0,1}.
exit_code_propagation.2: Federation usage error (bad flag, unknown lint id, --files with empty list).
exit_code_propagation.3: Orchestration error (one of the lints itself crashed — distinct from violation; flagged as 'lint_error' in stderr).

standalone_precedent_compliance.rule_from_32: Each lint stays standalone — federation is a thin orchestrator; never inlines or subsumes lint logic.
concrete_constraints: Federation MUST exec each lint as a child process (`hexa run tool/<lint>.hexa <args>`).
Federation MUST NOT re-implement any pattern detection; only marshal args + parse sentinel last-line.
Each lint remains independently invocable with identical CLI semantics whether called directly or via federation.
Adding/removing a lint requires editing only the federation registry (a static [str] of lint_ids → file paths), never the lint itself.

options_considered.name: SKIP_LINT_FEDERATION=1 env
options_considered.reason: Defeats lint purpose — equivalent to --no-verify backdoor at federation layer; would also conflict with raw 13 spirit (hooks must be enforceable when run).

options_considered.name: --skip <lint_id>
options_considered.reason: Per-lint skip is honest (user names what they're disabling); orchestrator MUST emit __FEDERATION_SKIP__ <lint_id> reason=<flag> to stderr AND record skip in sentinel as `skipped=<csv>` field.

options_considered.name: --only <lint_ids>
options_considered.reason: Explicit positive selection (e.g. CI partition); not a backdoor.

options_considered.name: Inline silence annotations (per-lint, e.g. `// @date-n-guard:`)
options_considered.reason: Already supported by L1; carries author intent on the offending line; surfaceable via grep audit. Consistent with raw 66 v2 R1 spirit (sentinel + annotation co-located).

user_bypass_mechanism.hardened_sentinel_with_skip: __PRE_COMMIT_FEDERATION__ <PASS|FAIL> lints=<N> ran=<N_ran> violations=<M> failed_lints=<csv> skipped=<csv> mode=<fast_fail|report_all>
user_bypass_mechanism.honesty_note: ANY skip emits a stderr line `[federation] WARN: skip <lint_id> reason=<flag>` so the audit trail is unambiguous. There is no silent bypass.

no .git/hooks/* path written by this kick
federation orchestrator (when impl'd in a future cycle) MUST NOT write .git/hooks/*

raw_13_compliance_verification.manual_invocation_pattern: hexa run tool/pre_commit_federation.hexa --staged   (user-explicit, identical pattern to tool/raw_enforce.sh and tool/atlas_precommit_check.sh)
raw_13_compliance_verification.ci_invocation_pattern: GitHub Actions / GitLab CI / launchd job invokes the same CLI; CI is OUTSIDE the local .git/hooks/ surface so does not violate raw 13.
raw_13_compliance_verification.f5_acknowledgement: F5 ACKNOWLEDGED for raw 13 forbidden auto-install — design is FORBIDDEN to ship a hook installer; if a future cycle wants one, raw 13 must be explicitly lifted by the session author with a separate cycle citation.

tier_1_promotions: FEDERATION_PIPELINE_SPEC_FINALIZED: orchestrator role + invocation modes + sentinel + exit-code propagation + lint registry committed to spec.
RAW_13_HONORED_BY_DESIGN: zero hook auto-install paths; manual CLI + CI patterns documented as the only surfaces.
STANDALONE_PRECEDENT_PRESERVED (#32): orchestrator is a thin marshal; each lint independently invocable; registry-driven add/remove.
BACKDOOR_REJECTED_HONESTLY: SKIP_LINT_FEDERATION=1 explicitly named and rejected; --skip emits audit WARN + sentinel field.
7_LINT_CATALOG_NORMALIZED: uniform sentinel format `__<NAME>__ PASS|FAIL violations=<N> ...` across all 7 lints (existing 2 + designed 5).
tier_1_implemented: federation pipeline spec with 6 invocation modes + 2 execution modes + sentinel format + exit-code map + 4 standalone constraints + 7-entry lint registry
user-bypass honest analysis: 4 options considered, 1 REJECT + 3 ACCEPT with reasons + hardened sentinel field

self_apply.MT1_carry_forward_origin: Cited 6 parent ω-cycles by file path + tag (#12 #20 #28 #24 #25 #18 #32)
self_apply.MT2_F5_acknowledged: F5 ACKNOWLEDGED for raw 13 forbidden auto-install — design ships ZERO .git/hooks/* writes; future hook install requires raw 13 lift first.

honesty_disclosures: raw 13 is NOT literally text-cited in tool/state/n6/docs grep — claim of 'historical rejection' rests on consistent practice (zero active hooks, all enforcement tools document user-explicit invocation) rather than a single doctrine doc. If raw 13 has a canonical text source elsewhere (e.g. external policy registry, untracked file), that source was not enumerated.
lint_catalog LOC values for L3..L7 are estimates (TBD) — actual LOC will be set when each impl cycle ships.

L2 py_ban_guard.hexa does NOT currently emit a machine-parseable __PY_BAN_GUARD__ sentinel — federation orchestrator will need to either (a) parse stderr text 'BLOCK — R37/AN13/L3-PY' OR (b) wait for the proposed py-ban-guard-machine-sentinel-add cycle.
Federation orchestrator is DESIGN ONLY in this kick — its impl is a separate cycle (pre-commit-federation-orchestrator-impl) and is BLOCKED on L3..L7 existing.

CI invocation pattern (raw 13 escape hatch) assumes CI runs OUTSIDE local .git/hooks/ — true for GitHub Actions/GitLab CI/launchd; would NOT be true for a server-side post-receive hook (which would need separate raw 13 analysis).

Per-lint --skip flag is convenient for partitioning but, if abused, becomes a slow-motion backdoor — recommend a future audit cycle (federation-skip-frequency-audit) once orchestrator ships, to detect normalized-skip patterns.
